title: Identity, Authentication, Sessions, and Recovery · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for identity, authentication, sessions, and recovery contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in identity and session assurance model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.
Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence.
Define issuance, binding, rotation, idle and absolute expiry, revocation, concurrent sessions, device evidence, and secure transport.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Identity Entity
steps.instruction:
debug case — Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

Inspect the Identity Entity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Identity Entity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Identity Entity, keep Identity Entity connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Identity Entity, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Identity Entity, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Identity Entity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Identity Entity, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Identity Entity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One email address is a stable global person identity.

stimulus.title: Identity Entity case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One email address is a stable global person identity.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity. Artifact under review: identity and session assurance model.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Identity Entity, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Identity Entity record says identity and session assurance model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Identity Entity.

For inspect the failure record, apply Identity Entity without its stated preconditions, omit the identity and session assurance model trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Identity Entity for identity and session assurance model.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Return usable authentication failures and telemetry without account enumeration, credential leakage, or inaccessible friction.
